title: 3B3-888
content:
{{Top|fga|canon=7276}} {{Otheruses|3B3}} {{Droid |type=Corporate |image=[[File:Battle_droid_3B3-888.png]] |name=3B3-888 |homeworld= |birth= |death= |creator= |manufacturer= |line= |model=[[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]{{Film|I}} |class=[[Battle droid/Legends|Battle droid]]{{CCG|set=Theed Palace Limited|cardname=3B3-888}} |cost= |length= |width= |height=1.91 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]{{DB|droid|battledroid|battle droid}} — Based on standard [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] technical specifics |mass= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Masculine programming]]''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars Character Encyclopedia]]'' |pronouns=new |sensor=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |plating=White |armament=[[E-5 blaster rifle/Legends|E-5 blaster rifle]] |equipment= |affiliation=*[[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] **[[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]] }} {{Quote|Hands up.''"
"''My give up. My give up.|3B3-888 and Bombad General Jar Jar Binks|}} '''3B3-888''', also known as '''7276''', was a [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] who served in the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Droid Army]] of [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]]. When Gunray's forces [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invaded]] and occupied the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], the native [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]] responded by massing their own [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|army]] to combat the invaders. 3B3-888 was a part of the force dispatched to eliminate the Gungans, and the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] captured two enemy officers during the resulting [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|battle]]. 3B3-888 was later deactivated, like all other Federation droids on the planet, when the [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|Droid Control Ship]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] Naboo was destroyed. ==Biography== 3B3-888 was a [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] who served in [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray's]] [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Droid Army]]. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Gunray used the forces under his command to [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], as a sign of protest against the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate's]] newly ratified [[Senate Resolution BR-0371|taxation of trade routes]]. In short order, Gunray had his troops [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invade]] Naboo and occupy the planet. 3B3-888 was a part of the force stationed on the planet, under the command of [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]]. In response to the occupation, a [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|substantial force]] of the planet's native [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]] assembled at the [[Great Grass Plains/Legends|Great Grass Plains]], headed by [[Bombad General/Legends|Bombad General]] [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]], and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Tobler Ceel/Legends|Tobler Ceel]].[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization]] OOM-9 and a sizable army of [[Droid/Legends|droids]], 3B3-888 included, were sent to meet the Gungans and engage them, resulting in the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of the Great Grass Plains]]. The Gungans were eventually routed, and as they retreated, 3B3-888, aided by several other B1s, captured both Binks and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Roos Tarpals/Legends|Roos Tarpals]]. Shortly afterward, however, the [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|Droid Control Ship]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] Naboo and controlling all [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] on the planet surface was destroyed, resulting in the instantaneous deactivation of 3B3-888 and the rest of the Federation force. Naboo's leader, [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], later captured Gunray and reclaimed possession of the planet. ==Characteristics== 3B3-888 had [[Color/Legends|white]] plating, black [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]], and, like all B1 battle droids, stood 1.91 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. 3B3-888 wielded an [[E-5 blaster rifle/Legends|E-5 blaster rifle]] in combat against the Gungans. The droid also exhibited the ability to capture retreating enemies, which it implemented during the Battle of the Great Grass Plains. ==Behind the scenes== 3B3-888 made its first appearance in [[George Lucas]]' [[1999]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. The character was later identified in the ''[[Theed Palace Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]]. ==Appearances== *{{Film|I}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars: The Phantom Menace Cine-Manga|''Star Wars: The Phantom Menace'' Cine-Manga]] ==Sources== *{{CCG|set=Theed Palace Limited|cardname=3B3-888}} {{1stID|3B3-888}} *{{SW|url=news/the-trade-federation-and-neimoidians-a-history|text=The Trade Federation and Neimoidians: A History|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=3B3-888 |fr=3B3-888 |nl=3B3-888 |pl=3B3-888 }} [[Category:B1-series battle droids]] [[Category:Droids of the Trade Federation]] [[Category:Droids with masculine programming]] [[Category:Droids with unspecified pronouns]]
